description: Granular save points for the full planning and construction lifecycle. Each save point must be completed and verified before dependents begin. Status: pending | in_progress | complete | blocked.

priority.critical: Blocks all downstream work. Must be done first.
priority.high: Needed for core functionality. Prioritise over medium/low.

status.complete: All acceptance criteria met and verified.
status.blocked: Cannot proceed — dependency unresolved or external blocker.

phases.name: Foundation & Repository Setup
phases.description: Create all scaffolding and shared tokens before any code is written.

savepoints.name: Repository Initialised
tasks: Create private GitHub repository: nse-ai-platform
Initialise root README.md describing the project
Create .gitignore covering: .env*, node_modules/, __pycache__/, .next/, dist/, venv/, *.pyc, .DS_Store
Create root directory structure: frontend/, api/, workers/, supabase/, .github/workflows/
Commit savepoints.json to project root
Create develop branch; enable branch protection on main (require PRs, require CI green)
Add CODEOWNERS file mapping /workers/ to backend owner and /frontend/ to frontend owner
acceptance_criteria: GitHub repository accessible and private
Directory structure matches spec in Section 16.8 of architecture doc
savepoints.json present at repository root
.gitignore covers all build artifacts and secret files
Branch protection active on main — direct pushes rejected
savepoints.notes: This is the very first action. Nothing else can start until the repo exists.

savepoints.name: Environment Variables Documented
tasks: Create root .env.example listing all 8 required environment variables with placeholder values and comments
Create api/.env.example for Node.js-specific vars
Create workers/.env.example for Python-specific vars
In README, add 'Environment Setup' section explaining each variable and where to obtain it
Tag each variable as: PUBLIC (safe for frontend) or PRIVATE (server-only)
acceptance_criteria: .env.example lists: SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, RESEND_API_KEY, JWT_SECRET, DATABASE_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL
SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY clearly marked PRIVATE
No real credential values exist anywhere in the repository
README Environment Setup section complete and accurate

savepoints.name: Design Tokens Defined
tasks: Create frontend/tokens/colors.ts: brand colors, signal colors (BUY/HOLD/SELL), impact severity, sentiment, chart palette, neutral scale, dark mode overrides
Create frontend/tokens/typography.ts: font families (sans/mono/data), full size scale, weight scale, tabular-nums config
Create frontend/tokens/layout.ts: 4px base spacing scale, responsive breakpoints, sidebar/topbar dimensions, content max-width
Create frontend/tokens/elevation.ts: 6-level shadow scale
Create frontend/tokens/animations.ts: duration, easing, reduced-motion overrides
Create frontend/tokens/index.ts re-exporting all tokens
Configure tailwind.config.ts to consume all token values via CSS variables
Create frontend/styles/globals.css with light and dark CSS variable definitions
acceptance_criteria: BUY signal: green-700 on green-100 — contrast ratio ≥ 4.5:1 (WCAG AA) — verified with contrast checker
SELL signal: red-700 on red-100 — contrast ratio ≥ 4.5:1 — verified
HOLD signal: amber-700 on amber-100 — contrast ratio ≥ 4.5:1 — verified
Dark mode CSS variables defined and toggled correctly by adding 'dark' class to <html>
No hardcoded hex color values outside tokens/ directory
tailwind.config.ts extends theme with all token categories

savepoints.name: CI/CD Pipelines Configured
tasks: Create .github/workflows/test.yml: python-tests job, node-tests job, e2e-tests job (as per Section 20.7)
Create .github/workflows/security.yml: npm audit, pip-audit, TruffleHog secret scanning (Section 21.9)
Create .github/workflows/daily-digest.yml: cron '30 15 * * 1-5' — runs send_digest.py
Create .github/workflows/weekly-review.yml: cron '0 16 * * 0' — runs send_weekly.py
Add GITHUB_SECRETS placeholders in workflow files with clear TODO comments
Add workflow status badges to README.md
acceptance_criteria: test.yml triggers on push to main and develop
security.yml triggers on push to main AND on schedule (Mondays)
daily-digest.yml triggers at 15:30 UTC weekdays (= 18:30 EAT)
weekly-review.yml triggers at 16:00 UTC Sundays (= 19:00 EAT)
All workflow files valid YAML (no syntax errors)
README shows CI badge

phases.name: Database & Supabase Setup
phases.description: Establish PostgreSQL schema, RLS policies, seed data, and auth configuration.

savepoints.name: Supabase Project Created
tasks: Create Supabase project named 'nse-ai-platform' at app.supabase.com — choose region closest to Kenya (eu-west-1 or similar)
Record SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_ANON_KEY in password manager
Record SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY in password manager — treat as highly sensitive
Record DATABASE_URL (direct) and POOLER_URL (pgBouncer port 6543) separately
Install Supabase CLI: npm install -g supabase
Run: supabase login && supabase link --project-ref [ref-id]
Verify supabase/config.toml generated with correct project ref
acceptance_criteria: Supabase dashboard loads at app.supabase.com/project/[ref]
supabase status output shows correct project name and region
All four connection strings securely stored in password manager
supabase/config.toml present in repository with project_id populated
outputs: Four connection strings in password manager

savepoints.name: Schema Migrations Written
tasks: Write supabase/migrations/001_create_tables.sql — all 10 tables: companies, stock_prices, financials, news_articles, detected_events, analysis_results, portfolio_allocations, signal_evaluations, users, user_preferences, email_logs
Write supabase/migrations/002_create_indexes.sql — all 9 indexes from Section 7.2 plus GIN index on raw_context
Write supabase/migrations/003_rls_policies.sql — all RLS policies from Section 21.4 covering all 10 tables
Write supabase/migrations/004_seed_companies.sql — INSERT for all 20 NSE companies with ticker, name, sector, Yahoo Finance ID in description field
Write supabase/migrations/005_audit_log.sql — audit_log table creation plus REVOKE UPDATE, DELETE for all roles
Write supabase/migrations/006_macro_indicators.sql — macro_indicators table for CBK rate, FX, CPI storage
Validate SQL syntax locally: psql [local-test-db] -f migrations/001_create_tables.sql
Apply migrations: supabase db push
acceptance_criteria: supabase db push completes with 0 errors
All 11 tables visible in Supabase Table Editor
All foreign key relationships correct — companies.ticker is FK target for stock_prices, financials, news_articles, analysis_results, signal_evaluations
RLS is ON for every table (visible in Supabase Auth Policies dashboard)
20 seed company rows present in companies table
audit_log INSERT succeeds; UPDATE on audit_log fails with permissions error

savepoints.name: RLS Policies Tested
tasks: Create two test users in Supabase Auth: test-viewer@example.com (viewer role), test-analyst@example.com (analyst role)
Test 1 — viewer SELECT on stock_prices: expect 200 rows returned
Test 2 — viewer INSERT to analysis_results: expect permission denied error
Test 3 — viewer SELECT from audit_log: expect 0 rows or permission denied
Test 4 — viewer A SELECT from user_preferences where user_id = viewer B: expect 0 rows
Test 5 — service_role INSERT to analysis_results: expect success
Test 6 — service_role INSERT to audit_log: expect success; UPDATE on same row: expect denied
Document all 6 test results in supabase/tests/rls_test_results.md
acceptance_criteria: All 6 RLS isolation tests produce expected results
Cross-user data leakage test returns exactly 0 rows
Service role write access confirmed working
audit_log immutability confirmed (UPDATE denied even for service_role)
Test results document committed to repository

savepoints.name: Supabase Auth Configured
tasks: Enable email/password authentication in Supabase Auth > Providers
Set minimum password strength to 'medium' (8+ chars, mixed case + number)
Add Site URL: http://localhost:3000
Add Redirect URLs: http://localhost:3000/auth/callback and https://[vercel-domain]/auth/callback
Configure email templates: confirm signup, reset password, magic link (use platform branding)
Create first admin user via dashboard; run: UPDATE users SET role='admin' WHERE email='[admin-email]'
Enable Supabase Realtime for tables: detected_events, analysis_results
Set email OTP expiry to 3600 seconds (1 hour)
acceptance_criteria: Test login with viewer account succeeds — JWT returned
Test login with wrong password fails — 400 returned, not 500
Admin user exists in both auth.users and users table with role='admin'
Realtime enabled for detected_events and analysis_results (visible in Supabase Realtime dashboard)
Email templates customised with NSE AI Platform branding
outputs: Admin account credentials in password manager

phases.name: Python Workers — Data Collection
phases.description: All Python ingestion scripts with full validation and test coverage.

savepoints.name: Python Environment Configured
tasks: Create workers/requirements.txt with pinned versions: supabase==2.x, yfinance==0.2.x, pandas==2.x, pydantic==2.x, httpx==0.27.x, tenacity==8.x, structlog==24.x, pandas-ta==0.3.x, anthropic==0.28.x, python-dotenv==1.0.x, psycopg2-binary==2.9.x, feedparser==6.x, beautifulsoup4==4.x, pdfplumber==0.11.x
Create workers/.python-version file with content: 3.11
Create workers/pyproject.toml with project name, version, and pytest configuration
Create workers/conftest.py with shared pytest fixtures
Create workers/services/db.py: factory function returning Supabase client from env vars
Create workers/services/ai.py: factory function returning Anthropic client from env vars
Create workers/models/ directory (empty __init__.py)
Test: python3 -c 'import supabase, yfinance, pandas, anthropic, pydantic' — no errors
acceptance_criteria: pip install -r requirements.txt completes with no conflicts
python3 --version returns 3.11.x
All 12 imports succeed in Python REPL
services/db.py returns a working Supabase client when valid env vars present
services/db.py raises a clear ValueError when env vars missing
pytest --collect-only runs with 0 errors (even with no tests yet)

savepoints.name: Pydantic Models Defined
tasks: Create workers/models/prices.py: StockPriceRecord — validators: ticker uppercase, close_kes > 0, price within 30% of 30-day avg (optional runtime check)
Create workers/models/news.py: NewsArticleRecord — headline required, source_url unique key, sentiment_score between -1.0 and 1.0
Create workers/models/financials.py: FinancialsRecord — period_type in ('annual','interim'), all monetary values in KES
Create workers/models/events.py: DetectedEvent — impact_score 0-100 enforced, event_type validated against allowed enum
Create workers/models/analysis.py: AnalysisResult — signal in ('BUY','HOLD','SELL'), confidence_score 0.0-1.0, key_drivers max 3 items
Create workers/models/portfolio.py: PortfolioAllocation, PortfolioHolding — weight_pct > 0, holdings list non-empty
Create workers/tests/unit/test_models.py — minimum 5 test cases per model (valid, invalid price, invalid signal, boundary values, null handling)
acceptance_criteria: StockPriceRecord(ticker='scom', close_kes=24.5) raises ValidationError (lowercase ticker)
StockPriceRecord(ticker='SCOM', close_kes=-1.0) raises ValidationError (negative price)
DetectedEvent(impact_score=101) raises ValidationError
AnalysisResult(signal='STRONG BUY') raises ValidationError
AnalysisResult(confidence_score=1.1) raises ValidationError
AnalysisResult(key_drivers=['a','b','c','d']) raises ValidationError (max 3)
pytest tests/unit/test_models.py — all tests pass

savepoints.name: Price Fetcher Implemented & Tested
tasks: Implement workers/data/fetch_prices.py — class PriceIngestionService with method run(tickers: list[str]) -> dict
Use yfinance.download() with period='1d', interval='15m' for intraday; period='1d', interval='1d' for EOD
Apply StockPriceRecord validation to every row before insert
Use Supabase upsert with on_conflict='ticker,price_date' to prevent duplicates
Add tenacity retry decorator: 3 attempts, exponential backoff 4-60s on HTTPError
Add structlog logging: ticker, rows_inserted, rows_rejected, duration_ms per run
Add __main__ block for direct script execution
Write workers/tests/unit/test_price_validation.py — test all StockPriceRecord validators
Write workers/tests/integration/test_price_ingestion.py — mock Supabase, mock yfinance
acceptance_criteria: python workers/data/fetch_prices.py runs without errors (requires valid env vars and market hours OR mocked data)
Duplicate price for same ticker + timestamp does NOT create a second row
Price of -1.0 is rejected and counted in rows_rejected, not inserted
Structured log output includes ticker, rows_inserted, duration_ms
Unit tests: 100% line coverage on validation logic
Integration tests: mock Supabase upsert called exactly once per valid row
Retry logic: 3 retries with backoff on HTTPError — verified with mock

savepoints.name: News Fetcher Implemented & Tested
tasks: Implement workers/data/fetch_news.py — class NewsIngestionService
Implement _fetch_rss(url: str) using feedparser — parse title, link, summary, published
Implement _scrape_nse_announcements() using httpx GET + BeautifulSoup4 table parsing
Implement _sanitise_text(text: str) — strip HTML tags, decode HTML entities, normalise Unicode, truncate to 2000 chars
Implement _associate_ticker(text: str) -> str | None — match article text against company name/ticker dictionary
Use source_url UNIQUE constraint for deduplication (upsert with on_conflict='source_url', do_nothing=True)
Set is_processed=False on all new articles (sentiment analysis runs separately)
Write workers/tests/unit/test_news_parsing.py — test sanitise_text, ticker association with sample text
Write workers/tests/integration/test_news_ingestion.py — mock HTTP responses, verify DB calls

HTML tags stripped from article body: '

Hello world

' → 'Hello world'
HTML entity decoded: '&' → '&', '<' → '<'
Text 'Safaricom CEO' maps to ticker 'SCOM'; 'KCB Group' maps to 'KCB'
Second run with same article URLs inserts 0 new rows (no duplicates)
All unit and integration tests pass

Implement _load_manual_csv(filepath: str) as primary ingestion method
Implement _parse_pdf_report(url: str) using pdfplumber as secondary method
Apply FinancialsRecord Pydantic validation to all rows
Upsert with on_conflict='ticker,period_end,period_type'

acceptance_criteria: After running ingestion, SELECT COUNT(*) FROM financials ≥ 20 rows (5 companies × 4 periods)
All revenue_kes, ebitda_kes, net_profit_kes, eps_kes populated for each row
Duplicate insert for same ticker+period produces 0 extra rows
FinancialsRecord correctly rejects negative EPS for companies not in loss
Unit tests pass

savepoints.name: Macro Indicators Fetcher Implemented
tasks: Add macro_indicators table migration: id, indicator_name, value, unit, period_date, source, created_at
Implement workers/data/fetch_macro.py — class MacroIngestionService
Implement _fetch_cbk_rate() using httpx GET on cbk.go.ke monetary policy page + BeautifulSoup4 parsing
Implement _fetch_fx_rate() using Open Exchange Rates API (free tier)
Implement _fetch_cpi() using KNBS data source or manual CSV fallback
Seed macro table with last 12 months of CBK rate, USD/KES, and CPI data from public sources

USD/KES rate within 1% of live interbank rate
CPI data present for at least last 6 months

savepoints.name: All Data Worker Tests Green
tasks: Run full data worker test suite: cd workers && pytest tests/ -v --cov=. --cov-report=html -m 'not e2e'
Achieve ≥ 80% overall coverage; 100% on all validation logic
Fix any failing tests or coverage gaps
Configure pytest markers: unit, integration, e2e
Add python-tests job to test.yml GitHub Actions workflow
Verify: GitHub Actions python-tests job passes on push
acceptance_criteria: pytest exits code 0 — all tests pass
Coverage report shows ≥ 80% on workers/ directory
Validators at 100% line coverage
GitHub Actions python-tests job passes in CI

phases.name: AI Workers — Events, Analysis & Portfolio
phases.description: Event detection engine, AI analysis pipeline, and portfolio allocation engine.

savepoints.name: Event Detection Engine Implemented
tasks: Implement workers/events/detect_events.py — class EventDetectionService
Implement calculate_price_z_score(prices: list[float], window: int = 20) -> float
Implement calculate_volume_z_score(volumes: list[int], window: int = 20) -> float
Implement detect_price_anomaly(ticker: str) — fetch last 21 sessions from DB, compute z-score, insert event if |z| > 2.5
Implement impact escalation: base_impact + 15 if volume_z_score > 2.0, capped at 100
Implement classify_news_event(article: NewsArticle) -> DetectedEvent | None using claude-haiku-3-5 with JSON-enforced prompt from Section 4.3
Implement LLM circuit breaker class from Section 16.6 — open after 5 failures, recover after 5 min
Insert all detected events to Supabase detected_events table
Write workers/tests/unit/test_event_detection.py — all 8 test cases from Section 20.2
Write workers/tests/integration/test_event_pipeline.py — mock prices + mock AI API
acceptance_criteria: Price jump of +25% on stable baseline triggers PRICE_ANOMALY with impact_score ≥ 40
Simultaneous volume spike adds exactly 15 to impact_score (or caps at 100)
LLM classifier returns valid JSON for 5 different news article fixtures
Impact score never exceeds 100 — verified across 20 parametric test cases
Circuit breaker opens after 5 consecutive mock AI failures
Circuit breaker auto-recovers after recovery_timeout
All unit tests pass; integration tests pass with mocked dependencies

savepoints.name: Technical Analysis Module Implemented
tasks: Implement workers/analysis/technicals.py — class TechnicalAnalysis
compute_rsi(prices: pd.Series, period: int = 14) -> dict — return value (0-100) + label (overbought/oversold/neutral)
compute_macd(prices: pd.Series) -> dict — return macd_line, signal_line, histogram, crossover label
compute_moving_averages(prices: pd.Series) -> dict — SMA50, SMA200, crossover type (golden/death/none)
compute_bollinger_bands(prices: pd.Series) -> dict — upper, middle, lower, price position label
compute_obv_trend(prices: pd.Series, volumes: pd.Series) -> str — rising/falling/neutral
compute_technical_score(ticker: str) -> float — aggregate 0-100 score from all indicators
Write workers/tests/unit/test_technical_analysis.py — use fixture price series with known RSI/MACD values
acceptance_criteria: RSI calculation for known 14-period test data matches expected value within ±0.5
MACD bullish crossover correctly identified in test data where MACD crosses above signal line
Golden Cross detected when 50-day SMA is above 200-day SMA in test data
Bollinger Band position: price at lower band labelled 'lower_band_touch'
technical_score stays within 0.0 to 100.0 for all test cases
Unit tests pass with ≥ 95% coverage on technicals.py

savepoints.name: Fundamental Analysis Module Implemented
tasks: Implement workers/analysis/fundamentals.py — class FundamentalAnalysis
compute_valuation_metrics(ticker: str) -> dict — P/E, P/B, EV/EBITDA, FCF yield, dividend yield
compute_profitability_metrics(ticker: str) -> dict — ROE, ROA, net margin, EBITDA margin
compute_growth_metrics(ticker: str) -> dict — YoY revenue growth, EPS growth, dividend growth
compute_leverage_metrics(ticker: str) -> dict — D/E ratio, current ratio, interest coverage
compute_sector_medians(sector: str) -> dict — median for each metric across sector peers
compute_fundamental_score(ticker: str) -> float — weighted aggregate vs sector medians
Handle missing data: return partial score if ≥ 50% of metrics available; return None if < 50%
Write workers/tests/unit/test_fundamental_scoring.py with fixture financial data
acceptance_criteria: P/E ratio = price / eps_kes — verified against manually calculated expected value
ROE = net_profit / equity — verified
Sector median computed from ≥ 3 companies; gracefully skips if fewer available
fundamental_score returns None (not 0) when < 50% of data present
fundamental_score stays within 0.0 to 100.0
Unit tests pass

savepoints.name: Sentiment Analysis Module Implemented
tasks: Implement workers/analysis/sentiment.py — class SentimentAnalysis
score_article(article: NewsArticleRecord) -> float — call claude-haiku-3-5 with structured prompt, parse JSON response, return score -1.0 to 1.0
score_unprocessed_batch(ticker: str, limit: int = 50) — fetch unprocessed articles, score each, update sentiment_score + sentiment_label + is_processed=True in DB
compute_aggregate_sentiment(ticker: str, days: int = 7) -> dict — weighted average (recency weight: 1/(days_ago + 1), NSE filing weight: 1.5×)
Return dict with: score, article_count, positive_count, negative_count, neutral_count
Write workers/tests/unit/test_sentiment.py — use 5 fixture articles with known expected sentiment
acceptance_criteria: Article fixture 'Company reports 25% profit increase' scores ≥ 0.5 (positive)
Article fixture 'Company issues profit warning' scores ≤ -0.3 (negative)
Article fixture 'Company appoints new operations director' scores between -0.1 and 0.2 (neutral)
NSE official filing gets 1.5× weight vs general news in aggregate
Aggregate score stays within -1.0 to 1.0
is_processed=True set on all articles after scoring
Unit tests pass

savepoints.name: Full AI Analysis Engine Implemented
tasks: Implement workers/analysis/run_analysis.py — class AnalysisEngine

build_context(ticker: str) -> dict — assemble: company profile, last 4 quarters financials, technical snapshot, sentiment aggregate, last 30-day events (sorted by impact desc), current macro indicators
build_prompt(context: dict) -> str — construct structured prompt from Section 5.2 schema with JSON-only instruction
call_ai_api(prompt: str) -> dict — call claude-sonnet-4-5 with temperature=0.2, parse JSON response, validate with AnalysisResult Pydantic model
run(ticker: str) -> AnalysisResult | None — orchestrate full pipeline; return None on API failure
run_all(tickers: list[str]) — iterate all tickers, log summary at end
Skip signal generation if confidence_score < 0.5 (store result but set signal='HOLD')
Write workers/tests/integration/test_analysis_pipeline.py — mock all external calls (AI, DB), test orchestration logic
acceptance_criteria: run('SCOM') completes end-to-end in < 30s (mocked AI API) and returns valid AnalysisResult
Returned signal is always in ('BUY', 'HOLD', 'SELL') — never null or other string
confidence_score always between 0.0 and 1.0
key_drivers list contains exactly 1-3 items
Run with mocked AI returning invalid JSON: function returns None, does not raise exception
Run with 20 tickers: all 20 complete without aborting (individual failures are caught)
Integration tests pass

savepoints.name: Portfolio Engine Implemented
tasks: Implement workers/portfolio/run_portfolio.py — class PortfolioEngine
get_active_buy_signals(min_confidence: float = 0.65) -> list[AnalysisResult] — query analysis_results for latest BUY signals
compute_raw_weights(signals: list) -> dict[str, float] — weight = confidence × (1 + fundamental_score/100)
apply_sector_cap(weights: dict, cap: float = 0.35) -> dict — iteratively reduce over-weight sectors
apply_stock_cap(weights: dict, cap: float = 0.20) -> dict — cap any single ticker at 20%
apply_cash_buffer(weights: dict, buffer: float = 0.10) -> dict — scale all weights to leave ≥ 10% cash
normalise_weights(weights: dict) -> dict — ensure sum = 100.00% within 0.01% tolerance
insert_allocation(allocation: PortfolioAllocation) — upsert to portfolio_allocations
Write workers/tests/unit/test_portfolio_allocation.py — all 5 test cases from Section 20.2
acceptance_criteria: Banking sector test case: 3 high-confidence banking stocks → sector allocation ≤ 35%
Single-stock dominance: 1 stock with 0.99 confidence → weight capped at 20%
Cash buffer always ≥ 10% of total portfolio
Total weights (holdings + cash) = 100.00% ± 0.01%
With 0 BUY signals: outputs 100% cash allocation (graceful empty case)
All 5 unit tests pass

savepoints.name: Signal Evaluation Engine Implemented
tasks: Implement workers/evaluation/evaluate_signals.py — class SignalEvaluator
get_evaluable_signals(min_age_days: int = 30) -> list — fetch signals older than min_age_days not yet evaluated
evaluate_signal(analysis_id, ticker, signal, signal_date) -> SignalEvaluation — fetch price performance for 30/60/90 days after signal_date
classify_outcome(price_change_pct: float, signal: str, threshold: float = 0.05) -> str — return SUCCESS/NEUTRAL/FAILURE
compute_aggregate_metrics() -> dict — overall accuracy, by confidence band, by sector, Sharpe ratio, max drawdown
Upsert to signal_evaluations table
Write workers/tests/unit/test_signal_evaluation.py
acceptance_criteria: BUY signal followed by +8% price move → outcome_60d = 'SUCCESS'
SELL signal followed by +8% price move → outcome_60d = 'FAILURE'
Price flat (< 2% move) → outcome = 'NEUTRAL' regardless of signal
Aggregate metrics computed correctly from fixture evaluation data
Unit tests pass

phases.name: Node.js Backend API
phases.description: Secure REST API with full auth, validation, rate limiting, and test coverage.

savepoints.name: Fastify Server Scaffolded
tasks: Initialise api/ as Node.js project: cd api && npm init -y
Install dependencies: fastify@4, @fastify/cors, @fastify/rate-limit, @supabase/supabase-js, zod, postgres, node-cache, pino
Install dev dependencies: vitest, supertest, @types/node, tsx, typescript
Create api/src/server.ts — buildApp() factory function (testable, not auto-starting)
Create api/src/index.ts — start server on PORT from env (default 3001)
Register plugins: CORS (strict allowlist), rate limit (60/min unauthenticated), pino logger
Register auth plugin: api/src/plugins/auth.ts — JWT validation using Supabase public key
Add GET /health endpoint returning DB connectivity status
Write api/src/routes/__tests__/health.test.ts — test 200 and 503 cases
acceptance_criteria: npm run dev starts server on port 3001 without errors
GET /health returns { status: 'healthy', timestamp: '...' } with 200
GET /health returns 503 when Supabase unreachable (mock DB failure)
Unauthorized request to any future protected route returns 401 (auth plugin registered)
CORS: request from http://evil.com returns 403; request from http://localhost:3000 returns 200
Health test passes in Vitest

savepoints.name: All API Routes Implemented
tasks: Implement GET /stocks — list companies with optional ?sector= filter; returns data + meta envelope
Implement GET /stocks/:ticker — company profile + latest close price from stock_prices
Implement GET /stocks/:ticker/prices — OHLCV data with ?from=&to=&interval= params
Implement GET /signals — latest signal per ticker with ?signal=&min_confidence=&sector= filters
Implement GET /signals/:ticker — full signal history for one ticker with ?limit= param
Implement GET /analysis/:ticker — most recent full AnalysisResult row for ticker
Implement GET /events — detected events with ?ticker=&type=&min_impact=&limit= filters
Implement GET /portfolio/latest — most recent portfolio_allocations row
Implement GET /portfolio/history — all allocations (admin role required)
Implement GET /news — news articles with ?ticker=&limit= filter
Implement GET /performance — signal evaluation aggregate metrics
Implement POST /users/preferences — save watchlist and alert preferences to user_preferences
Apply Zod schema validation to all query params and path params
Apply JWT auth guard to all routes except /health
acceptance_criteria: All 12 routes respond 200 with correct data shape when authenticated
All routes respond 401 when no Authorization header provided
GET /signals?signal=INVALID responds 400 with RFC 7807 error body
GET /stocks/XXXXXX responds 404 with RFC 7807 error body
SQL injection attempt in ticker param returns 400 (Zod rejects non-alpha chars)
GET /portfolio/history returns 403 for analyst role (admin-only route)
Response envelope always has: data, meta (with total/page/per_page), generated_at, request_id

savepoints.name: API Tests Written & Passing
tasks: Write test files for all 12 routes following pattern in Section 20.3
Test: 200 success path with mock Supabase data

savepoints.name: Railway Cron Workers Deployed
tasks: Create railway.toml in workers/ directory with cron job definitions for all 8 scheduled scripts

Cron schedules (UTC): fetch_macro='0 3 * * *', poll_prices='5-25 6-13 * * 1-5' (every 20min), fetch_news='*/30 6-14 * * 1-5', detect_events='35 13 * * 1-5', run_analysis='30 13 * * 1-5', run_portfolio='0 14 * * 1-5', evaluate_signals='30 14 * * 1-5', fetch_financials='0 20 * * *'
Deploy Python workers service to Railway from /workers directory
Set all environment variables in Railway dashboard
Monitor first scheduled execution of each script in Railway logs
Verify stock_prices table receiving new rows during NSE trading hours
